name: Space Channel 5 VR Kinda Funky News Flash!

reviews.review_description: Ok game fun like dance eden best

reviews.review_title: It will remind you of the Dreamcast days
reviews.review_description: The game is a bit strict when it comes to detecting the proper "pose", but other than that I was happy with my purchase. If you have a crush on Ulala, then you definitely MUST buy this game. It was worth it just to see her up close.

In case you were scared off by recent reviews, the game works perfectly on my Quest 3S. It's so much fun! Unfortunately, the game is short and can be completed in just an hour, but I'm glad the game was a little more complex than the old games; when you're asked to "Pose!", you really have to watch what the choreography is because there's at least 3 different variable poses.

Also different from the old games, your life bar is 3 hearts and you have to get 3 moves in a row wrong to get a Game Over, so that's basically impossible to fail a level. It's amazing to see the characters in VR, so if you enjoyed the old games, you need to get this one.
If you are a fan of the original, this is a must.

I just bought this app and it's not working at all! It won't open. It's showing cloud sync then doesn't open no matter how many times I've tried. Did I pay for nothing? What can I do ? I want to play this app and it doesn't open.

This game was a part of my childhood and the moment I saw it I downloaded it and started playing so as you can imagine my sadness when there was ONLY 4 chapters I’m sure that you guys can make the whole play through for the faith and trust of your customers. BTW I played all four already and I was very happy and it was fun I’ve had it for like an hour 😩

Have a vood time every one,!
This game is goooooooood

reviews.review_title: Cool but too expensive for what you get
reviews.review_description:
Lovely experience but WAAAAAAY too short for the price. Bought it for my girlfriend and she completed the campaign in literally 34 minutes (we were timing her exercise by chance). Not the full Dreamcast game, only 4 levels. Very disappointing considering the price.

The arcade mode is just a port of a Japanese arcade game (I presume) as has no English dialogue but provides English subtitles. Once again, would have been acceptable in a £8 game but this was a far cry from the full experience we were expecting for £18.99.

TL/DR: Must try harder SC5vr

Intro:

Before I get into details about this game, I want to throw this out there. Growing up, I played Space Channel 5 on the PS2, and was hooked by its retro style and wacky rhythm gameplay. The unique characters having dance battles in outer space was right up my alley, as I absolutely adore rhythm games. Plus, Ulala steals the show when she is on screen.

Overview:

Space Channel 5 VR is a love letter to fans of the series. This is the first time we have seen a new entry in the franchise in many years. The gameplay is the same as before. You must perfectly copy the enemies dance moves in a Simon Says fashion. Of course, since this is VR, you will actually be using your arms instead of buttons on a controller. Timing is crucial and the game introduces a new mechanic of matching enemies poses on top of already mimicking their arm directions.

Modes:

The game has a few different modes to play. Story mode takes you through 4 chapters (45 minutes or so). You can also replay these on Hard difficulty. Arcade Mode has a remixed version of the first chapter in story mode, as well as the Hatsune Miku DLC (Comes bundled for free). And finally, Trial mode, which is a 10 minute long dance battle.

Extra:

You can visit the dressing room to change Ulala's costumes, read up on some character bios, and also visit View Mode.

Final Thougts:

I love this game. It has the charm of the originals and really gets you moving. Ulala and the rest of the gang looks better than ever, and the music is really catchy. My only problem is that it's so short. If you combine story, the Miku DLC, and the dance trial, the game is roughly an hour or so long. That being said, the game has a bit of replay value for score chasing and the added Hard difficulty, which I definitely loved. There is so much style and I really would love for them to add more into the future. It really feels like you're hanging out on a T.V. set with Ulala. This is a must have for any Space Channel 5 fan. It's kinda funky in a very good way. I'm sure Ulala will make a cosmic return someday. 

For now, Stay Tuned :)
Story mode is admittedly short, but it's the closest thing to being a true magical girl in VR so far! I had so much fun!

reviews.review_title: The perfect way to play Space Channel 5!
reviews.review_description: This is what I always wished the Dreamcast version had been! Just hitting buttons got a little old...dancing is so much more fun! And it seems to really do a good job at picking up your movements. The main story can be beaten in a few hours time, but there seems to be a lot of other stuff to do also. Really glad I purchased.

reviews.review_title: I’m a huge SC5 fan but I gotta be honest
reviews.review_description:
This game is incredible immersive. The first scene got me so excited because it was an ode to the OG game. The characters, graphics and dancers were just what I needed for a 2020 VR version. It also hit a soft spot to see old characters make a return. I was ready to go on a journey and be fully immersed in my childhood. However......

I beat the game in 30 minutes. I beat all levels without even trying my best. It was just way too easy and short. I understand that VR games involve a lot of software engineering so I’m probably asking for too much but at least make the game $10 then. 

When the credits rolled I was confused and conflicted because they shouted out the fans in the credit. I want to support but I also want a refund :-(

This game was my childhood and finally seeing the vr experience come to oculus I knew I had to get my share of sweet nostalgia.

Although the game has its downs.First of which being the short story.I loved how they combined the awsome memories I had for the game into a story that works perfectly but when it was over i felt a little...underwhelmed. Yeah I could go and try and get a perfect viewer rate but playing the story again wasnt as exhilarating as the first time through. 

Furthermore I would love to see more content that is unique on it's own right.The Miku DLC was fine but it just felt like a re-skin with a couple bonus moves you could do.I want originality, excitement, twist and turns that spark my inner childhood.

Overall if you like space channel 5 then buy the game it has all the cheesy lines and dance moves that were in the original games and it brings challenge.

(Okay Beyond this point is an edit just so I can add on my point)

I have been looking at the reviews for this game and most have been fairly positive (Aside from the technical issues)

Although i believe my point to be slightly bias ,based on he fact that this game stuck with me growing up, I can understand how people may not like the game or even be stuck on buying the game or not.

So I took it onto myself to try and look into this game with a fresh pair of eyes and honestly I dont think that's how the game is supposed to be viewed.

When looking at the game again I found it very.....cheesy but that how it's supposed to be. It wants you to laugh and play along and the addition to this game coming out on vr make it that more convincing that you actually a part of the environment. It wants you to feel the nostalgia it wants to make you laugh and make you think "I missed this"

Honestly I cant 100% enforce you to buy the game if you dont know what space channel 5 is but if you do and you liked those titles then yes buy the game! I had a blast playing through it and although it being a tad short I'm always going back to it.

So I just got the game and I passed the whole game in like an hour and a half 😆! Is a really fun game, but i think is kind of short, and it only comes with one Miku song. Hope they add more chapter later on...

reviews.review_title: The worst game I have ever played
reviews.review_description:
It is astounding that this game was released. There is literally NOTHING worth recommending here. 

I am so angry that it even exists that I can barely type! 

Quite literally the worst game I have played on any platform, ever.

The game is nicely made, and I enjoyed the travel back in time, as I played it on the PS2. But it is way to short to be worth the money. You can only play around 30 minutes an half of the material is not even in English. Also it repeats itself and is way too easy. I would not buy it again and regret spending the money.

I finished it within 45 minutes of play.
I've loved this game since I saw the amazing launch installation at E3 many many years ago.
Was super excited to play it again and wondered if they were going to include the Michael Jackson Easter egg like the Sega game. Nope. 
Still super disappointed that it was so short.
I hope they update with more game play or I'd rather get a refund.

reviews.review_title: Super weird but fun and short
reviews.review_description: It's very weird and has a fun and weird story however I would not pay this much money for this short of a story there is hatsune miku and it is a goofy world to revist time and time again and remind syou what can be done with VR

reviews.review_title: Up, Down, Up, Down, Chu, Chu, Chu
reviews.review_description:
Another from the Dreamcast/UGA stable.
SC5 had always been a guilty pleasure of mine so had waited on this since it was announced earlier in the year.

Basically the game was one of the first 'Simon Says' style rhythm games - This has translated really well to the Quest. It does a very good job of sitting you in the world of our favourite Pole Dancetastic news reporter of the future, Ulala.

The issue if any is its a short ride to the end of the game. With its 4 main levels and 1 bonus Hatsuni Miku challenge. 
The further challenges in there are quite replayable, but not core to the game. 

I will recommend this to any SC5 fan - but you may feel a bit short changed on what you get for your money.
It would truly fare better with some added DLC, but I have a feeling that its unlikely to see it.

reviews.review_title: It does work on my Quest 3
reviews.review_description:
While it doesn’t launch every time, it eventually works if you try enough times. For me it only took 3 tries max to get the game to launch and most times it works on first try. Definitely needs a fix but at least I can play the game normally without much trouble.

The game itself is real fun. Visually it’s a treat, the characters are great and everything about it is quite catchy. It is too short but it makes up for it with replayability. It’s basically like an arcade game in terms of length and content.

I had a great time with it.
